result:
Security audit complete. ✅

## Summary

I ran the **Harden** security audit loop over the entire repository (`ENTRY_POINT=./`, `OUTCOME_TYPE=pr`) and opened a PR with the fixes.

**PR:** https://github.com/thabungm/testHubLaunch/pull/36 — `harden: security audit ./ (2026-06-25)`

### What was scanned
- `.github/scripts/*.sh` (8 scripts), `.agents/skills/diagnose/scripts/*.sh`, `ralph-run.sh` (3345 lines), `.vscode/`, config & markdown.
- `npm/pnpm audit` is **N/A** — this is a shell-script repo with no JS manifest.

### Findings → fixes
| Severity | Issue | Location | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| Medium | Predictable temp file in world-writable `/tmp` (CWE-377 / CWE-59) | `hula-verify-gather.sh:116` | ✅ Fixed |
| Medium | Predictable temp file in world-writable `/tmp` (CWE-377 / CWE-59) | `hula-verify-post.sh:63` | ✅ Fixed |

Both scripts wrote to attacker-predictable paths (`/tmp/hula-verify-diff-${PR_NUMBER}.patch`, `/tmp/hula-verify-comment-${PR_NUMBER}.md`), enabling symlink-following clobber / info disclosure on shared hosts. Replaced with `mktemp "${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/...-XXXXXX..."` — the exact convention already used throughout `ralph-run.sh`, so downstream consumers (which read the returned path) are unaffected.

### Reviewed and found safe (no change)
- `eval "$CMD"` in `ralph-run.sh` runs repo-controlled regression commands from `ralph.md` — intended behavior.
- GitHub token is redacted from remote URLs before embedding in PR/issue bodies, and single-quoted in the git credential helper.
- `.github/scripts` validate numeric args, reject `..` traversal, and JSON-escape output. No hard-coded secrets.

### Verification
- `bash -n` passes for **all 10** shell scripts.
- `mktemp` template tested live (produces unpredictable path).
- No new critical issues introduced; `shellcheck` is not installed in this environment, so static checking relied on `bash -n`.

Lessons file updated at `.hublaunch/lessons/harden-scan-202606250941_RALPH_LESSONS.md`.
